title: Attached Component Instances
text: Attached Component Instances Component instances are said to be attached to the calling component instances when they are created using the /attached switch with the newinstance command, or when the Attach to Parent widget property is set to False . Attached component instances are deleted in a two-phase process when the calling component exits: Before any instances are deleted, all instances must agree to the close request. ProcScript in the quit or accept trigger of every instance determines whether the instance agrees to the close request. After all instances have returned 0 in $status while processing the quit or accept trigger, the instances are closed by deleting them one by one. The two-phase close mechanism is available for both Accept and Quit actions. It is started when the Quit or Accept operation is called, or the quit or accept trigger is fired, of a form instance that has attached child instances. Depending on the original request (Accept or Quit), the accept or quit trigger of all involved instances is fired, starting with the child instances and then from the bottom up to the instance that originated the close request. If one of the involved instances leaves the accept or quit trigger with a negative value in $status (for example, -1 ), it indicates that it does not agree, and the close sequence is canceled without closing any of the involved instances, even if some instances have already agreed to the close request. During the second phase, no quit or accept triggers are fired anymore. If defined, the cleanup operation of every instance being deleted is fired. Two-Phase Close in Tab Widget The two-phase close is especially useful when using the tab widget. By definition, tab forms are attached to a parent form containing the tab widget. For example, the following tabbed dialog box consists of a form component instance containing the tab widget, and 7 attached tab component instances. The two-phase close ensures that all attached tab instances are allowed to be closed before proceeding to delete instances. All the tab form instances are deleted before the parent form. For example, you can add ProcScript to the quit trigger to remind the user of unsaved data. Related Links newinstance Creates a new instance of a component. Attachment Controls whether the component instance runs in attached mode, meaning that it is created by another component and is deleted when the parent component instance is deleted. trigger accept Handles processing when an Accept request is received, usually with the intention of closing the component and keeping all changes made on it. This trigger is exposed as an operation in the component signature. trigger quit Trigger that handles processing when a Quit request is received, usually with the intention of closing the component without saving any modifications made in it. This trigger is exposed as an operation in the component signature. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
